name: SPLENIC INFARCTION
dominant_finding: initially ill-defined hypoechoic lesion ← inflammation, edema, necrosis
distribution: single / multiple focal wedge-shaped peripheral defects
discriminating_features: single / multiple focal wedge-shaped peripheral defects
initially ill-defined hypoechoic lesion ← inflammation, edema, necrosis
later increasingly well-defined echogenic lesion ← organization of infarct with fibrosis
mottled area of increased attenuation on NECT ← hemorrhage

general: single / multiple focal wedge-shaped peripheral defects
US: initially ill-defined hypoechoic lesion ← inflammation, edema, necrosis
later increasingly well-defined echogenic lesion ← organization of infarct with fibrosis
mottled area of increased attenuation on NECT ← hemorrhage
large focal hyperattenuating lesion on CECT
focal progressively more well-demarcated areas of decreased attenuation without
size decreases + attenuation returns to normal

clinical.Cx: acute febrile illness, abscess formation, pseudocyst formation, splenic rupture, hemorrhage
